title: saviniona insularis
side_effects: Saviniona insularis can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches, which can be caused by consuming large quantities of its leaves or extracts. Long-term use of the plant's essential oils may also lead to allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. In severe cases, improper use of saviniona insularis can cause kidney damage, heart palpitations, and even seizures due to the toxic compounds present in the plant. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using saviniona insularis or any other herbal remedy to avoid these potential side effects.
